input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the approach for runtime theme switching in .NET MAUI:

## 1. Define theme ResourceDictionaries

Create separate XAML files for each theme:

```xml
<!-- Themes/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

Repeat for `DarkTheme.xaml` and `HighContrastTheme.xaml` using the same keys with different values.

## 2. Merge a default theme in App.xaml

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <resources:LightTheme />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

## 3. Create a theme-switching service

```csharp
public static class ThemeService
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(string theme)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        mergedDicts.Add(theme switch
        {
            "Dark"         => new DarkTheme(),
            "HighContrast" => new HighContrastTheme(),
            _              => new LightTheme()
        });

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("AppTheme", theme);
    }
}
```

## 4. Wire it up in your settings page

```xml
<Picker x:Name="ThemePicker"
        ItemsSource="{Binding Themes}"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedTheme}" />
```

```csharp
partial void OnSelectedThemeChanged(string value)
    => ThemeService.ApplyTheme(value);
```

## 5. Restore on startup (in App.xaml.cs)

```csharp
public App()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(Preferences.Get("AppTheme", "Light"));
}
```

## Key points

- Use `{DynamicResource KeyName}` (not `StaticResource`) in your XAML so controls pick up changes immediately when the dictionary is swapped.
- Keep resource keys identical across all three dictionaries.
- `Preferences` persists the choice across launches.

This gives you instant, app-wide theme switching with no restart required.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking how to set up themed support in a .NET MAUI app with three branded themes (light, dark, high-contrast) that can be switched at runtime from a settings page. This is a general "how-to" question, not a code change request against a specific repository. I'll provide a concise explanation with the key architectural pieces.
